extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out -> not sent) holds on the entire reachable state space. The only transition that sets sent:=true while opted_out is true is t4, guarded by (opted_out and acc==28). However acc==28 is unreachable. Each step event updates acc to (3*acc + k) mod 48 for k in {8 (step_0), 38 (step_1), 17 (step_2)}; the nested if-then-else is exactly mod-48 reduction (verified: 3*acc+k <= 3*47+38 = 179 < 4*48, so at most 3 subtractions, equivalent to mod 48). Starting from acc=0, BFS over the acc dynamics yields only {0,2,5,8,11,14,17,20,23,26,29,32,35,38,41,44,47}. All nonzero reachable values are congruent to 2 mod 3 while 28 == 1 mod 3, so acc never equals 28; t4 is dead code. Consequently every send_attempt while opted_out falls to t3, which sets sent:=false. Additionally, t1 (optout) sets sent:=false, and all step transitions set sent:=false; only t2 sets sent:=true and it requires not opted_out. A full reachable-state model check of the complete (opted_out, sent, acc) state machine enumerated 51 reachable states with zero violations, and confirmed no reachable state has opted_out true with acc==28. Since the reachable set is closed (finite fixpoint reached), the property holds for executions of any length, including the bounded 50-event horizon.
